response: Upstairs the publisher planned the book's structure. Each section must have a unique number of puzzles. The first section contains twelve puzzles. The second section doubles the first section's count. The second section has twenty-four puzzles. The third section adds five more than the second. The third section has twenty-nine puzzles. The total number of puzzles adds up. The entire book has sixty-five puzzles. * * * The editor carefully arranged the puzzles. The first section had twelve puzzles. The second section had twenty-four puzzles. The third section had twenty-nine puzzles. The total number of puzzles is sixty-five. The publisher approved the final count. The book will contain sixty-five puzzles. * * * The puzzles are now ready for publication. The sections are correctly structured. The total number of puzzles is sixty-five.
